read: This is the concrete TileLang response to the raw selected-row write-volume failure. The kernel keeps the query-major MFMA contribution body, maps packed LI slots back to original selected slots with SourceSelected, accumulates by compressed group inside the TileLang CTA, then emits final [group,D] dKV atomics only after the in-CTA reduction.

implementation.contract: q,dO [S,H,D] bf16; probs,scaled_dP [S,K,H] bf16 in original LI selected-slot order; SourceSelected/PairSlots/TileOffsets/ActiveGroups static route metadata; output [G,D] fp32

comparison.read: The selected-limit TileLang body finally beats raw selected-row stores because it reduces by compressed group before global memory. The source-indexed path is below the old sparse-atomic wall, while pack+kernel is only borderline because it still materializes and repacks P/scaled-dP.

next: Reuse or produce LI route metadata on device instead of rebuilding the selected-limit plan in the bridge.
Fuse or adjacent-produce P and scaled-dP so the dKV producer does not require 512 MiB probs plus 512 MiB grad-logit slabs at S4096.
Connect final [G,D] dKV to the compressor/LI backward consumer while preserving the no-selected-state saved-tensor contract.
